q: How does blood clotting seal a cut in the skin?
options: Platelets release histamine, causing vasodilation and flooding the wound with plasma proteins
Damaged tissue and platelets release clotting factors that trigger a cascade converting fibrinogen to fibrin, forming a mesh that traps red blood cells into a clot
White blood cells form a physical plug at the wound site before any chemical signals are sent
Collagen fibers contract to close the wound, and plasma proteins harden around them
explanation: Vascular damage exposes collagen, activating platelets and a coagulation cascade; thrombin converts soluble fibrinogen into insoluble fibrin strands that form the structural mesh of a clot.
